mappings.rationaleAI: Design, construction, and maintenance of public infrastructure corresponds to construction of civil engineering works (B), including dams (B.1) and bridges (B.3).
Portland cement composites including reinforced and prestressed concrete as building materials (A.3.i) and concrete construction techniques (A.6.c).
Construction from brick, stone, and concrete block corresponds to bricks and tiles (A.3.d), stone (A.3.e), and masonry construction techniques (A.6.b).
Civil engineering structures spanning obstacles to carry loads correspond to bridges as civil engineering works (B.3).
Design and maintenance of mechanical systems connects to construction machinery (A.5) and auxiliary systems including HVAC (A.7.g.ii).
Permanently enclosed structures correspond to general building construction (A) and building components (A.7).
Iron as a building material (A.3.g) used in structural construction.
Barriers impeding water flow with arch, gravity, and embankment typologies correspond to dams as civil engineering works (B.1).
Design and construction of habitable structures connects to preconstruction planning and design programming (A.1) and building components (A.7).
Applied practice solving technological problems underpins both general building construction (A) and construction of civil engineering works (B).
Wood as lumber building material (A.3.c) and wood and timber construction techniques (A.6.a).
Steel as a construction metal (A.3.g) and steel construction techniques (A.6.d).
Engineered artificial waterways correspond to aqueducts (B.2) and harbour and hydraulic works (B.5).
Copper as a construction metal (A.3.g) used in plumbing, roofing, and electrical wiring.
Naturally occurring solid mineral masses used as stone building material (A.3.e).
Metals including iron, steel, aluminum, and copper as construction materials (A.3.g).
The science of metals underpins the properties and performance of metallic construction materials (A.3.g).
Heating, ventilating, and air-conditioning systems (A.7.g.ii) incorporate refrigeration as a core building services technology.
Interior and exterior lighting systems as building auxiliary systems (A.7.g.iv).
Full lifecycle of delivering built assets from planning through demolition corresponds to both general building construction (A) and civil engineering works (B).
Military constructions including bunkers and fortified structures connect to underground construction (B.4).
Wheels as components of construction transport machinery (A.5.a).
Machines applying forces to perform work correspond to construction machinery (A.5) including transport and lifting equipment.
